name: Cipaille or Cipate (Layered Meat Pie)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 ts Mixed ground cloves, nutmeg, cinnamon, allspice
recipeInstructions:
Traditionally this layered pie is best made with game. Failing a supply of venison or pheasant, it can be made with a mixture of meats and poultry as in this recipe.
Servings: 8 to 10
1. Cut chicken, beef, and pork into 1-inch cubes and place in a large bowl. Combine with onions; cover and refrigerate for at least 12 hours or overnight.
2. Arrange salt pork evenly in the bottom of a 3-quart casserole, preferably cast iron with a lid. Layer with about one-third of the meat mixture and one-third of the potatoes. Season with about one-third of salt, pepper, and spices.
3. Roll out half of the pastry slightly thicker than for a normal pie and arrange on the potato layer, cutting a small hole in the center. Repeat with two more layers of meat and potatoes seasoned with salt, pepper, and spices.
4. Slowly add enough chicken stock through the hole until liquid appears. Cover the dish and bake in a preheated 400°F oven for 45 minutes or until the liquid simmers.
5. Reduce temperature to 250°F and continue to bake, covered, for 5 to 6 hours more or until the top crust is a rich golden brown.
